revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鉢塚古墳 (藤井寺市)]]
{{Coordinates List|section="Baizuka"}} [[File:Hachizuka Kofun (Fujiidera), zenkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachizuka Kofun Mound}}{{small|Frontal section on the left, rear circular section on the right.}}]]
Archaeologically, one kofun near the {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|qq=}} is presumed to be its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}{{Sfn|Chūai Tennōryō Kofun|2014|pp=pp1-124}}:
There are no official designations by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} for the {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (satellite mounds) of this kofun. * **Hachizuka Kofun** (鉢塚古墳) ** Location: 4-chōme, Fujiidera, Fujiidera City ({{Coord|34|34|4.75|N|135|35|43.20|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|name=Hachizuka Kofun}}) ** Shape: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ** Size: Mound length of 60 meters ** Historic Site Designation: National Historic Site, part of the "Hachizuka Kofun" in the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" ** Description: Located north of the rear circular section of the Oka Misanzai Kofun{{Sfn|Chūai Tennōryō Kofun|2014|pp=pp1-124}}. It is a small keyhole-shaped tumulus with its frontal section facing west. No evidence of fukiishi (covering stones) has been found{{Sfn|Chūai Tennōryō Kofun|2014|pp=pp1-124}}. A surrounding moat has been identified, though it is now buried<ref name="Hachizuka and Warizuka Kofun"/>. Details about the burial facility or grave goods remain unknown<ref name="Hachizuka and Warizuka Kofun"/>. Based on the cylindrical haniwa excavated, the mound is estimated to have been built during the same period as the Oka Misanzai Kofun, and it is associated as a baizuka of the latter{{Sfn|Chūai Tennōryō Kofun|2014|pp=pp1-124}}.
Additionally, the Lostuka Kofun, once located directly in front of the frontal section of the Oka Misanzai Kofun (now destroyed), is also theorized to have been a baizuka<ref name="Hachizuka and Warizuka Kofun">[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/kunifusiteibunkazai/1387696968186.html Hachizuka Kofun and Warizuka Kofun] (Fujiidera City Homepage).</ref>. This was a circular mound with a diameter of 20 meters, but it disappeared around {{ill|1965|en|1965|qid=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40) without prior investigation, leaving its details unclear{{Sfn|Chūai Tennōryō Kofun|2014|pp=pp1-124}}.
Around the area, the Warizuka Kofun (a National Historic Site) and the {{ill|Oka Kofun|ja|岡古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oka Kofun|qq=}} (now destroyed) are located near the eastern corner of the frontal section of the Oka Misanzai Kofun. These mounds date to a period about a century earlier than the Oka Misanzai Kofun{{Sfn|Chūai Tennōryō Kofun|2014|pp=pp1-124}}<ref name="Hachizuka and Warizuka Kofun"/>. Furthermore, records from {{ill|William Gowland|en|William Gowland|qid=Q3568666|lt=William Gowland|1=William Gowland}} around 1877 (Meiji 10) mention a total of seven mounds, including the four previously mentioned. However, the details of the additional mounds remain unclear{{Sfn|Chūai Tennōryō Kofun|2014|pp=pp1-124}}. It is noteworthy that while over ten baizuka were constructed during the earlier {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|qid=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun|1=Ichinoyama Kofun}} phase, the number decreased significantly during the Oka Misanzai Kofun phase{{Sfn|Chūai Tennōryō Kofun|2014|pp=pp1-124}}.
== References ==
